findings.text: Domain-specific EfficientNet-B0 fine-tuning (Secure Triplet Loss) produced a statistically significant face recognition improvement.
ISO/IEC 30136 unlinkability satisfied only after deployment-domain adaptation; public-dataset-only models did not comply.
Face recognition preserved discriminative capacity under variable environmental conditions.
findings.metric: FNMR = 0.00% in both controlled & uncontrolled settings

findings.text: Hierarchical gate fusion reduced system FMR to 0.0000%; fingerprint gate blocked all impostors before face processing; cross-modal independence confirmed.
Gas execution across all four smart contract operations was deterministic; no unbounded execution paths found.
All 22 access control guards rejected adversarial transactions with EVM reverts; no unauthorized state change on deployed ledger.
Enrollment latency dominated by scanning hardware, not software; sensor accounted for the majority of P95 total.
findings.metric: Sensor: ~38.6 s of 47.1 s P95 · AI + anchoring: 13.8 s

findings.text: Verification latency acceptable for controlled entry.
IPFS throughput penalty in async ingestion mode is protocol-bound, not hardware-bound; negligible in consensus mode (absorbed by QBFT ceiling).
Firth's Penalized Logistic Regression confirmed a statistically significant Architecture × State interaction; DID resolution fails before biometric processing under revoked credentials.

conclusions.conclusion: Multimodal-hierarchical biometric subsystem eliminates false accepts under live adversarial conditions and satisfies ISO/IEC 30136 unlinkability. Elevated FNMR (31.5%) is a deliberate and known cost of the conservative fingerprint gate operating point.
Permissioned QBFT layer delivers deterministic execution, unanimous state consistency under fault injection, and scalable ingestion. The ~30 TPS consensus ceiling is an intrinsic protocol property.

Smart contract guard surface rejects all tested adversarial vectors without exception. DID-as-addressing mechanism structurally prevents biometric template retrieval for revoked identities. Zero plaintext biometric exposure confirmed across all system layers.

Integrated system operates within acceptable latency and resource bounds under live conditions. Consensus integrity survives crash fault injection with autonomous recovery. System remains viable under zero-infrastructure wireless deployment without performance degradation.

H₀ rejected. Architecture × State interaction is statistically significant. Credential revocation in the proposed system prevents both unauthorized access and biometric template processing. In decoupled architectures, revocation has no effect on authorization outcome, a structural enforcement gap closed by hierarchical coupling at the data-path level.

recommendations.title: Fingerprint FNMR Reduction
recommendations.context: FNMR of 31.50% is the most operationally significant limitation.
directions: Develop adaptive quality thresholds to reduce genuine rejection rates

recommendations.title: Validator Scalability Assessment
recommendations.context: DID/VC framework validated on a 4-node permissioned cluster.
directions: Evaluate architecture under larger validator sets and higher simultaneous user populations
Characterize impact of validator count on QBFT finality latency
Systematically study on-chain nonce registry behavior under concurrent enrollment traffic

recommendations.title: IPFS Throughput Optimization
recommendations.context: The −76.5% ingestion penalty in async mode is protocol-bound by per-object content-addressing overhead.
directions: Evaluate content-addressed storage alternatives with batched pinning
Investigate lazy propagation or dedicated IPFS cluster configurations
Target recovery of ingestion throughput in burst enrollment scenarios without sacrificing off-chain storage guarantees

recommendations.title: Demographic & Environmental Generalizability
recommendations.context: Study enrolled 50 participants from a single institutional context.
directions: Replicate live cohort evaluation across demographically and environmentally diverse populations
Assess generalizability of operational FMR and FNMR values
Cross-institutional deployment to evaluate portability of permissioned network configuration and DID resolution mechanism

recommendations.title: Formal Cross-Contract Desynchronization Detection
recommendations.context: Cross-contract desynchronization was detected and remediated during adversarial testing.
directions: Investigate formal on-chain desynchronization detection mechanisms
Implement cross-contract consistency checks executed atomically within critical state transitions
Move from administrative remediation to structural prevention of the identified attack surface

recommendations.title: Cross-Domain Applicability
recommendations.context: Architecture evaluated exclusively in an attendance monitoring context.
directions: Evaluate credential-anchored template retrieval model across these modalities
Identify domain-specific adaptations required by different biometric capture environments and credential lifecycle policies
